response.justification: The candidate brings a genuinely relevant geospatial background (founder of the GISCollective open-source geo-data platform, long collaboration with Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, where he built asynchronous, high-throughput services in DLang. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust) in commercial settings, and his C++ exposure is limited to a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project — C++ does not even appear in his skills list, and he has no Rust. He also lacks direct routing, navigation guidance, or tile-pipeline experience, and his last five years have been TypeScript/Node product work. Worth a conversation only if the team is open to ramping a systems-capable polyglot onto C++.
strengths: Founded and operates an open-source geospatial data platform (GISCollective) used by Green Map, showing real GIS domain commitment
Low-latency distributed-systems experience at Sociomantic: async I/O, caching, fibers on services handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering-craft culture: TDD, CI/CD, test-tooling authorship, and Linux/Docker/Kubernetes operational experience
concerns: Modern C++ experience is brief and a decade old; no Rust; C++ is absent from his current skills list
No experience with routing engines, navigation guidance, map-tile generation, or comparable data pipelines
Recent five-plus years are TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, a significant distance from systems-level pipeline engineering
